Under Pusya come barley, wheat, rice, sugarcane, forests,
ministers, kings, fishermen and the like, virtuous men and those that
arc engaged in big and small sacrifices.
To Asle^a are assigned artificial things, bulbs, roots, fruits,
insects, reptiles, poison, robbers, cereals and all classes of physicians.
[In Ayurveda many types of Cikitsa-treatment—such as Salya,
Salfikya, Kayacikitsa etc. arc mentioned.]
(
The following arc ruled by Magha : People rich in gold
(money) and corn, granaries, mountaineers, those who are devoted
to parents or the maneSy traders, heroes, carnivorous beings and
women-haters.
[The commentator takes the word Kosthggara to mean Vipu-
lagramah, large villages.]
gmrvr^rrfq wtn
The following are presided over by Purvapkalguni : Actors,
young women, amiable persons, musicians, artists, commodities,
cotton, salt, honey, oil and boys.
